ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general potato pancake class (FOODON_00002482); no specific rosti-with-sausage entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP covers potato-based dishes broadly; no rosti-specific code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept for rosti with sausage identified in published SNOMED CT release.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of rosti with sausage is approximately 200 g, comprising one rosti patty (~130 g) and one grilled sausage (~70 g).

nutrient_source.title: Rosti with Sausage — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from HPB Foodo entry for 'Rosti with sausage' (cr_id F14060014). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for rosti with sausage exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 72 is estimated using boiled/mashed potato (GI ~72–78, University of Sydney GI Database) as the closest analogous carbohydrate base, consistent with pan-fried shredded potato preparation. The sausage component contributes negligible carbohydrate and does not materially alter the GI. GL per serving calculated as: (72 × 17.09 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 200 g serving) / 100 = 24.6.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 632 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold; driven by added salt in the rosti and processed sausage content.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit rosti with sausage; if consumed, restrict to a maximum portion of 80 g and choose a low-sodium sausage alternative, as the sodium load of 632 mg/100 g is strongly contraindicated for blood-pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI potato base (estimated GI 72) combined with 17.1 g carbohydrate per 100 g and only 2.3 g dietary fibre produces a high glycaemic load of ~24.6 per standard serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid regular consumption of rosti with sausage; if eaten, limit the portion to 80–100 g, substitute white potato with cauliflower or sweet potato to lower the GI, and pair with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 22.18 g/100 g with estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100 g and trans fat ~0.2 g/100 g from butter and processed pork sausage, combined with high sodium 632 mg/100 g, presents a compounded cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid rosti with sausage due to its high saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content; if consumed occasionally, choose a lean chicken or turkey sausage, prepare the rosti with olive oil instead of butter, and limit the portion to 80 g.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g), saturated fat (~7.5 g/100 g), processed pork sausage (arachidonic acid precursor), nightshade potato, and high sodium (632 mg/100 g) are all pro-inflammatory.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid rosti with pork sausage; substitute with a chicken or turkey sausage, replace butter with olive oil, and monitor individual response to potato as a nightshade vegetable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rosti with sausage is a Swiss-origin dish consisting of a pan-fried shredded potato cake (rösti) served alongside a grilled sausage, commonly pork-based. The rösti is prepared by grating raw or parboiled potatoes, seasoning with salt, and frying in butter or oil until golden and crispy on the outside with a soft interior. The sausage is typically grilled or pan-fried separately. The dish delivers 307 kcal per 100 g, with a high fat content of 22.18 g (including approximately 7.5 g saturated fat and 0.2 g trans fat from butter and processed sausage), moderate protein at 9.96 g, and 17.09 g carbohydrate with only 2.33 g dietary fibre. Sodium is notably high at 632 mg per 100 g, driven by both added salt and the processed sausage. The estimated glycaemic index is 72 (high), yielding a glycaemic load of approximately 24.6 per 200 g serving. Clinically, this dish is unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension (sodium exceeds 600 mg/100 g threshold), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI and GL), cardiovascular disease (high saturated and trans fat plus sodium), and rheumatoid arthritis (pro-inflammatory fat profile and nightshade potato). It is not halal-certified due to pork sausage content. The dish is popular as a breakfast or brunch item in Swiss and European cuisine and appears in Singapore food service contexts.
common_user_queries: How many calories in rosti with sausage?
Is rosti with sausage suitable for hypertension?
Swiss potato pancake with sausage calories

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo Singapore food composition database entry F14060014 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat and trans fat are estimated from typical butter and pork sausage composition consistent with USDA FoodData Central reference data for similar preparations. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using boiled/mashed potato as the closest approved reference.
notes: The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because the HPB Foodo database entry F14060014 does not individually report these values, and no sufficiently close substitute entry in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy or Foundation Foods provides a composite rosti-with-sausage panel as of 2026: monounsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported in HPB Foodo F14060014; Saturated fat value of 7.5 g/100 g and trans fat value of 0.2 g/100 g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for pan-fried potato cake with butter and pork sausage components and are flagged as estimates. This dish contains pork sausage and is not halal. The input record incorrectly tagged dietary_tags as 'vegetarian' and 'plant_based'; these have been corrected to false in dietary_flags. Rosti is a Swiss-origin dish; multilingual naming rules for Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese cuisines do not apply — local_names fields are null except for the German romanized form 'Rösti mit Wurst'.
